title: Expressions, Decisions, and Iteration · Guided Runtime Studio
summary: Route support cases through explicit rules and iterate changing record sets without truthiness or termination defects. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a transparent support-case routing engine while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict expression grouping, short-circuit evaluation, assignment, optional chaining, and nullish coalescing.
Use truthiness only when every falsy value shares the same domain meaning and otherwise write explicit guards.
Express mutually exclusive branches, guard clauses, and exhaustive fallbacks without unreachable paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the workshop evidence note for Loop Control: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Route tickets with priority 2, role "viewer", and maintenance=false through an explicit decision table. Then flip maintenance to true and prove exactly one branch owns the changed outcome.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use break, continue, labels, and early return without skipping cleanup or required records.
